description: In this episode, Joe and Jacob discuss the latest changes in Solana, including a new proposal by Anatoly SIMD45 for asynchronous program execution, which separates user transaction blocks from vote blocks to enhance performance and reduce latency. They also cover recent commits related to Geyser entry interfaces and the new BPF loader for program runtime v2, which allows for automatic program resizing without additional costs.
topic_summary: Overview of Solana's updates including asynchronous program execution proposal [t=9:36], Geyser entry interface improvements [t=120:86], and the new BPF loader [t=166:54].
